title: नरसिंह गायत्री (विस्तारित)
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह गायत्री (विस्तारित) ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
उग्रं वीरं महाविष्णुं ज्वलन्तं सर्वतोमुखम्।
नृसिंहं भीषणं भद्रं मृत्युमृत्युं नमाम्यहम्॥

॥ नरसिंह गायत्री - प्रथम रूप ॥
ॐ नरसिंहाय विद्महे
वज्रनखाय धीमहि
तन्नो नरसिंहः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ द्वितीय रूप ॥
ॐ उग्रनरसिंहाय विद्महे
ज्वालामालिने धीमहि
तन्नो नरहरिः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ तृतीय रूप ॥
ॐ नृसिंहदेवाय विद्महे
महाबलाय धीमहि
तन्नो नृसिंहः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ चतुर्थ रूप ॥
ॐ लक्ष्मीनरसिंहाय विद्महे
भक्तवत्सलाय धीमहि
तन्नो नारसिंहः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ नरसिंह मूल मन्त्राः ॥
ॐ क्ष्रौं नरसिंहाय नमः।
ॐ उग्रं वीरं महाविष्णुम्।
ॐ ह्रीं क्ष्रौं श्रीं नृसिंहाय नमः।

॥ नवनरसिंह नाम ॥
उग्रो वीरश्च नरसिंहो महाविष्णुर्ज्वलन्मुखः।
सर्वतोमुखभीषण्यो भद्रो मृत्युहरो हरिः॥

नवैतानि च नामानि नरसिंहस्य यः पठेत्।
सर्वग्रहभयान्मुक्तो भवेत्सम्पत्समन्वितः॥

॥ इति श्री नरसिंह गायत्री विस्तारित सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Gayatri (Extended) ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Ugram Viram Maha-Vishnum Jvalantam Sarvato-mukham.
Nrisimham Bhishanam Bhadram Mrityu-mrityum namamyaham.

|| Narasimha Gayatri - First Form ||
Om Narasimhaya Vidmahe
Vajranakhaya Dhimahi
Tanno Narasimhah Prachodayat.

|| Second Form ||
Om Ugra-narasimhaya Vidmahe
Jvalamaline Dhimahi
Tanno Naraharih Prachodayat.

|| Third Form ||
Om Nrisimha-devaya Vidmahe
Mahabalaya Dhimahi
Tanno Nrisimhah Prachodayat.

|| Fourth Form ||
Om Lakshmi-narasimhaya Vidmahe
Bhakta-vatsalaya Dhimahi
Tanno Narasimhah Prachodayat.

|| Narasimha Mula Mantrah ||
Om Kshraum Narasimhaya Namah.
Om Ugram Viram Maha-Vishnum.
Om Hrim Kshraum Shrim Nrisimhaya Namah.

|| Nava-Narasimha Nama ||
Ugro Virash-cha Narasimho Maha-Vishnur-jvalan-mukhah.
Sarvato-mukha-bhishanya Bhadro Mrityu-haro Harih.

Navaitani cha namani Narasimhasya yah pathet.
Sarva-graha-bhayan-mukto bhavet sampat-samanvitah.

|| Iti Shri Narasimha Gayatri Vistarit Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: नरसिंह गायत्री के चार रूप प्रस्तुत हैं: प्रथम - 'ॐ नरसिंहाय विद्महे, वज्रनखाय धीमहि' (वज्र जैसे नखों वाले नरसिंह), द्वितीय - 'ॐ उग्रनरसिंहाय विद्महे, ज्वालामालिने धीमहि' (ज्वालामाला से शोभित उग्र नरसिंह), तृतीय - महाबल नृसिंह, चतुर्थ - भक्तवत्सल लक्ष्मीनरसिंह। नव नरसिंह नाम - उग्र, वीर, नरसिंह, महाविष्णु, ज्वलन्मुख, सर्वतोमुख, भीषण, भद्र, मृत्युहर - इन नौ नामों के पाठ से सर्वग्रहभय से मुक्ति और सम्पत्ति प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Four Gayatri forms covering different aspects of Narasimha
Includes Ugra, Jwala, Mahabala and Lakshmi-Narasimha forms
Nine names of Narasimha free from all planetary fears
Contains powerful bija mantras for Narasimha sadhana
Invokes wealth and freedom from all graha doshas
description: Dedicated to Lord Narasimha, the Narasimha Gayatri Extended (नरसिंह गायत्री (विस्तारित)) is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Traditional, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to include Ugra, Jwala, Mahabala and Lakshmi-Narasimha forms, grant wealth and freedom from all graha doshas, and contain powerful bija mantras for Narasimha sadhana. For best results, devotees chant this on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Gayatri Extended in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narasimha Gayatri Extended with four forms and nine names in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful mantras for protection and graha shanti.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narasimha Gayatri (extended) mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Gayatri Extended (नरसिंह गायत्री विस्तारित) is a 24-syllable Vedic-meter mantra dedicated to Bhagavan Narasimha — the half-man half-lion fourth avatara of Vishnu who appeared from a stone pillar to slay the asura-king Hiranyakashipu and protect his devotee child Prahlada. The standard form runs: Om Vajranakhaya Vidmahe, Tikshnadamstraya Dhimahi, Tannah Narasimhah Prachodayat — 'Om, may we know the Diamond-Clawed One; may we meditate on the Sharp-Toothed One; may that Narasimha inspire and illuminate us.' The extended version includes additional Gayatri formulations for the multiple forms of Narasimha — Ugra, Jwala, Mahabala, and Lakshmi-Narasimha — making it a comprehensive Narasimha-upasana mantra. The mantra is preserved in classical Vaishnava tradition. (Source: Narasimha Tapaniya Upanishad and classical Vaishnava upasana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of chanting Narasimha Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Gayatri is regarded as one of the most powerful protective mantras in the Vaishnava canon. Devotees chant it for (1) fearlessness — Narasimha is the supreme abhaya-mudra deity; (2) removal of all graha-doshas including Kala Sarpa Dosha, Pitru Dosha, Mangal Dosha, Shani Dosha and Rahu-Ketu afflictions; (3) destruction of enemies — physical, psychic, and supernatural — as Narasimha tore Hiranyakashipu apart; (4) protection of children and family — recalling Prahlada's salvation; (5) freedom from fear of death (mrityu-bhaya) and untimely death (akala-mrityu); (6) wealth and prosperity, especially when chanted in the Lakshmi-Narasimha form; and (7) cure of black magic, evil eye (drishti dosha), and negative occult influences. The Beeja-mantra Kshraum and Hreem are often added for amplification. (Sources: Narasimha Purana; Ahirbudhnya Samhita Pancharatra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Narasimha Gayatri be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Gayatri is most powerfully chanted on Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi — the avatara day, falling around April-May), every Saturday (Shanivar — to mitigate Shani-related sufferings), every Swati nakshatra day (Narasimha's birth-star), every Chaturdashi tithi (the 14th lunar day — Narasimha's appearance tithi), during Sandhya hours (twilight — when Narasimha appeared, neither day nor night), and during periods of intense personal trouble or perceived attack. Recommended: 108 chants daily for 40 or 48 days as a Narasimha purashcharana, ideally seated facing south or west, with offerings of red flowers, til-oil lamp, and panakam (jaggery-water). Many Andhra-Telangana families perform monthly Narasimha homas using this Gayatri as the primary mantra. (Sources: Narasimha Jayanti vrata vidhi; Ahobilam tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Narasimha Gayatri different from regular Vishnu mantras?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Gayatri carries a uniquely fierce-protective vibration distinct from gentler Vishnu mantras. (1) Most Vishnu mantras (Vishnu Sahasranama, Om Namo Narayanaya, Hare Krishna) invoke Vishnu's saumya (gentle, peaceful) aspect — for liberation, devotion, and prosperity. (2) The Narasimha Gayatri specifically invokes the Ugra (fierce-protective) aspect of Vishnu — designed to slice through obstacles, evil influences, and existential fear. The mantra includes vajra (diamond-thunderbolt) and tikshna damstra (sharp fangs) imagery, marking it as a kavacha-style protective mantra. Vaishnava acharyas often recommend that those facing chronic obstacles, hostile environments, or psychic attacks first chant the Narasimha Gayatri to clear the field, then move to gentler Vishnu mantras for deeper sadhana. (Sources: Pancharatra Agama; Sri Vaishnava acharya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between the Narasimha Gayatri and Prahlada?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Gayatri is intrinsically connected to the story of Prahlada — the boy-bhakta whose unwavering devotion to Vishnu, despite being tortured by his asura-father Hiranyakashipu, caused Vishnu to incarnate as Narasimha and emerge from a pillar at twilight to slay the demon and protect the child. After Hiranyakashipu's death, Prahlada offered the great Narasimha Stuti (Bhagavata Purana 7.9) — among the foremost Vaishnava bhakti-stotras. The Gayatri-form is held to encapsulate the protective shakti that Prahlada invoked, and is therefore especially recommended when chanted on behalf of children, students facing exam stress, or anyone in a hostile family environment. The Ahobilam pilgrimage in Andhra Pradesh (Narasimha-Kshetra) preserves the unbroken parampara of Narasimha-upasana with this Gayatri. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.5–10; Ahobilam Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What other Narasimha mantras and stotras complement this Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Gayatri is most powerfully recited together with (1) Narasimha Stuti by Prahlada (Bhagavata Purana 7.9) — the foremost Narasimha-stotra; (2) Lakshmi Narasimha Karavalamba Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya — the deeply popular Shrimat Payonidhi-niketana hymn; (3) Narasimha Kavacha Stotram (Brahmanda Purana) — the protective armor recited daily by those facing graha-doshas; (4) Sri Narasimha Mahamantra Om Hreem Kshraum Ugram Viram Mahavishnum Jvalantam Sarvatomukham — the foremost Narasimha mula-mantra; (5) Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanama Stotra — the 108 names; (6) Narasimha Sahasranama from the Brahmanda Purana; and (7) the verse Ito Nrsimhah Parato Nrsimhah for total directional protection. A complete Narasimha sadhana combines Gayatri, Stuti, Kavacha, and the Mahamantra. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 7.9; Adi Shankara Karavalamba Stotra; Brahmanda Purana Narasimha Kavacha.)
